title: ArrayMesh builder for topology-modifying operations
description: Add factory/builder methods to ArrayMesh for operations that change topology: delete-vertices, delete-edges, merge-by-distance, join, linear subdivide. Each returns a new ArrayMesh from computed flat arrays without constructing a HalfEdgeMesh intermediate. ArrayMesh already has the right structure — dense float[] positions, int[] faceIndices, fixed vertsPerFace, lazy CSR topology via QuadMeshTopologyHelper. It is structurally identical to Blender's internal mesh representation. The gap is that there is no way to create a new ArrayMesh from a topology-changing operation without going through HalfEdgeMesh. This is the foundation for porting all node operations off HalfEdgeMesh, which is the primary cause of OOM and slowness at high subdivision levels.
definition-of-done: 1. ArrayMesh has static factory methods for: subdivideQuads(positions, faceIndices) -> ArrayMesh, deleteVertices(mesh, boolean[]) -> ArrayMesh, deleteEdges(mesh, boolean[]) -> ArrayMesh, mergeByDistance(mesh, float) -> ArrayMesh, join(meshA, meshB) -> ArrayMesh. 2. Each method operates on flat arrays without constructing HalfEdgeMesh. 3. Unit tests verify each operation produces correct geometry (vertex counts, face indices, topology). 4. Subdivision at level 6 on a cube produces correct vertex/face counts matching the HalfEdgeMesh implementation.
testing-plan: 1. For each factory method, create a test that applies the operation to a known mesh and verifies vertex count, face count, and a sampling of vertex positions. 2. Compare ArrayMesh subdivision output against HalfEdgeMesh subdivision output at levels 1-4 (mesh fingerprint). 3. Benchmark ArrayMesh subdivision at level 6 and verify it completes in <1s with <512MB heap.
todos: Implement ArrayMesh.subdivideQuads — flat array arithmetic for edge midpoints and face centroids
Implement ArrayMesh.deleteVertices — compact arrays, remap face indices
Implement ArrayMesh.deleteEdges — identify affected faces, rebuild arrays
Implement ArrayMesh.mergeByDistance — spatial hash, union-find on flat arrays
Implement ArrayMesh.join — concatenate position arrays, offset face indices
Write unit tests for each operation
Benchmark against HalfEdgeMesh at subdivision level 6
unknowns: Whether deleteEdges on ArrayMesh can maintain the uniform vertsPerFace invariant or needs to support mixed face sizes
Whether mergeByDistance needs the full CSR topology or can work from face indices alone
